meaning: n.阴影,影子,影像,预兆,庇护,隐蔽处,阴暗,少许vt.遮蔽,使朦胧,预示,尾随vi.渐变,变阴暗
examples: A shadow fell across her face.
The ship's sail cast a shadow on the water.
He has always lived in the shadow of his brother.
Shadow Treasury Minister Cathy Jamieson told the House that Labour would support the backbench motion.
Its early titles included Barbarian and Shadow of the Beast for the Amiga and Atari ST.
